EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving shortwave trough of low pressure moved through the northern Sierra from late on the 3rd through the morning hours of the 4th bringing with it gusty winds. Ridge gusts topped 104 mph while some wind prone areas along US Highway 395 in Washoe Valley peaked at 59-83 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mesonet atop Slide Mountain (elevation 9650 feet MSL) recorded a wind gust of 104 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Associated moisture and a weak upper-level trough moved through northern and central Nevada. This system brought light amounts across the valley areas and locally heavier snows across the mountainous regions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nine inches fell at Hole-In-Mountain station, site HIMN2, elevation 8161 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another fast-moving, but relatively weak storm system moved into the Sierra Nevada on the 25th and crossed east into central Nevada on the 26th. Snow levels were rather high with this system with the bulk of the accumulating snows falling above 7000 feet in the Carson Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: For the higher elevations of western Nevada, snowfall around the Carson Range and the northeastern Tahoe Basin was generally around 1 to 2 inches above 7000 feet MSL.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A stronger storm system moved across the Sierra Nevada and the Carson Range mainly on the 16th, with lighter snow showers and icy roads lingering into the morning of the 17th. This storm produced strong winds, but it also produced more significant precipitation that previous storms, mainly as snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: For western Nevada, snowfall around the Reno-Sparks metro area was generally 1 to 3 inches, except less than 1 inch on the valley floor.  Higher snowfall amounts around 4 inches occurred in the Surprise Valley. Around Carson City, Minden, and the higher foothills west of Reno, snowfall totals were between 4 and 8 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A trough of low pressure and its associated surface cold front moved into western Nevada from the northwest to the southeast during the evening hours of February 29th into the early morning hours of March 1st. The main impact ahead of the front on the 29th were strong gusty winds. Snow developed around sunset the 29th in northeastern California and moved south-southeast through the night.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mesonet station PVPN2 atop Peavine Peak (elevation 8270 feet MSL) recorded a wind gust of 75 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep Pacific low pressure system and a rich plume of moisture combined to bring heavy rain and localized flash flooding to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were approximately 12 to 15 swift water rescues in the Las Vegas Valley, including at least one which occurred on normally dry ground. Cheyenne Ave was closed between Pecos Rd and Lamb Blvd due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: As a coastal low tracks south, moisture brings heavy snow to mountain areas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A storm system brought strong gusty winds along with critical fire weather conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Public reported tree limbs 7 to 10 inches in diameter broke due to high winds as well as a helicopter blown over at the airport. Wind speed at Elko ASOS recorded an estimated gust in near 45 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A storm system brought strong gusty winds along with critical fire weather conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The public reported a treetop blown off of a tree at the golf course in Ely. KELY ASOS reported an estimated gust of 48 mph at the time.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed over the Mojave Desert in response to leftover heat plus a shortwave trough passing by. One storm produced damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds capsized a 20 foot boat in Swallow Bay. All 12 people on board were rescued.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A dust devil in the northeast part of the Las Vegas Valley caused damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A dust devil tore the roof and awning off a mobile home, and the debris smashed into two parked cars in front of the home next door.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving shortwave trough of low pressure moved through the northern Sierra from late on the 3rd through the morning hours of the 4th bringing with it gusty winds. Ridge gusts topped 104 mph while some wind prone areas along US Highway 395 in Washoe Valley peaked at 59-83 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mesonet station GLCNV atop the Galena Creek Bridge on Interstate 580 (elevation 5089 feet MSL) recorded a wind gust of 83 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow moving Pacific low pressure system brought widespread rain and high elevation snow to the Mojave Desert. Isolated heavy snow occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two sites between 8600 and 8900 feet near the town of Mount Charleston estimated 8 inches of snowfall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moving through the Mojave Desert helped lift an unstable air mass, producing scattered thunderstorms. Isolated severe weather occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One inch hail covered Highway 95 about 8 miles south of the Highway 93 interchange.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low moved through the west producing mountain snows.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Twelve inches of snow fell at Ward Mountain, Elevation 9152 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A four-corners high was set up over southwestern CONUS. This produced hot temperatures nearing or exceeding 100 with isolated thunderstorms in central and northern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The sustained wind was accompanied by blowing dust that reduced visibility to 0.75 SM or less. This wind was from a thunderstorm outflow. Station (RSRNV), 11.1 SW Winnemucca, Elevation 4350 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated to scattered showers and thunderstorms produced some gusty winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Station (BSPNV), 12.4 miles NE Wells, Elevation 6002 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An early start to convection on Tuesday had some thunderstorms in northern Nevada produce small hail, moderate rain rates in some areas as well as strong outflow winds. Moisture continues to be around on Wednesday with dew points in the 50's and 60's.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rock Spring Creek Raws (RSCN2), Elevation 5427 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A stronger storm system moved across the Sierra Nevada and the Carson Range mainly on the 16th, with lighter snow showers and icy roads lingering into the morning of the 17th. This storm produced strong winds, but it also produced more significant precipitation that previous storms, mainly as snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: For the higher elevations of western Nevada, snowfall around the Carson Range and the northeastern Tahoe Basin was generally around 8 inches near 7000 feet MSL and up to 15 inches above 7000 feet MSL. This snowfall occurred in a 24-hour period from 16 January 0700PST to 17 January 0700PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through the southern Great Basin and the Mojave Desert, bringing strong westerly winds ahead of it and strong northerly winds behind it.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds knocked out power to the town of Mount Charleston.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through the southern Great Basin and the Mojave Desert, bringing strong westerly winds ahead of it and strong northerly winds behind it.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The peak measured gust occurred 10 miles N of Mercury. Five power poles were blown down in Indian Springs, and two in Pahrump.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree fell into power lines, causing a power outage and an arcing transformer. Two street signs were blown down, a carport and gazebo were damaged, a large branch fell onto a carport at an apartment complex, and a business display sign was damaged. 2571 customers were without power.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Seven power poles were blown down in Mesquite, knocking out power to 1275 customers.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts of 30 to 40 mph blew a restaurant sign onto a vehicle in Henderson, blew down a light pole near Desert Inn and Decatur, and blew down a power pole near Flamingo and Highway 95, causing a power outage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain on the 22nd, followed by clearing skies overnight, led to isolated dense fog on the morning of the 23rd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dense fog reduced visibility to 1/8 mile 4 miles WNW of Pahrump.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A trough of low pressure and its associated surface cold front moved into western Nevada from the northwest to the southeast during the evening hours of February 29th into the early morning hours of March 1st. The main impact ahead of the front on the 29th were strong gusty winds. Snow developed around sunset the 29th in northeastern California and moved south-southeast through the night.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mesonet atop Slide Mountain (elevation 9650 feet MSL) recorded a wind gust of 94 mph. Several wind gusts in the previous and next 2 hours were over 70 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A trough of low pressure and its associated surface cold front moved into western Nevada from the northwest to the southeast during the evening hours of February 29th into the early morning hours of March 1st. The main impact ahead of the front on the 29th were strong gusty winds. Snow developed around sunset the 29th in northeastern California and moved south-southeast through the night.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An isolated wind gust event caused roof damage to at least two houses along with several trees being uprooted in the area of Bath Street and Pyrenees Street in western Carson City. The nearest anemometer to the area at Western Nevada College (mesonet WMC) had a wind gust to 30 mph closest to the time of the reported damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A vigorous, but dry, cold front swept south across western Nevada on the 8th. Most locations did not receive any precipitation with this front. The more impressive weather associated with the front was very strong east winds at the ridge tops in the wake of the frontal passage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: After a prolonged dry and warm autumn, the first appreciable snowfall of the season blanketed far western Nevada and the Sierra on the 8th into the 9th. This was associated with a cold front producing bands of snow that didn't not exhibit a rain-shadow. Peak snowfall rates of nearly 2 inches per hour were observed in Douglas County the morning of the 8th. Impacts from this snowfall were significant for travel however somewhat tempered due to it falling on a Sunday.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread snowfall reports of 4 to 6 inches in the Reno Sparks area, 3 to 5 inches in Carson City, and over 1 foot in Minden Gardnerville.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous reports of heavy snow across Smith Valley to Yerington, ranging from 8 to 16 inches. This was the 3rd heaviest single-day snowfall on record for Yerington coop site with 13 inches.

Still dry vegetation and high winds contributed to several major wildfires near Reno, NV and Walker, CA. A brief period of spillover rain and snow occurred that night into the morning of Nov 18.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pinehaven Fire in Caughlin Ranch area of SW Reno with wind gusts 72-77 MPH measured in the vicinity. On Tuesday, November 17, at approximately 1 p.m., a fire started on the hillside just south of Pinehaven Road and Sierra Pine Drive. Five homes were destroyed, three homes were heavily damaged, and 21 homes suffered minor damage from the fire.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Baccarat Fire started near Red Rock Road north of Reno on the 4th before being contained on the 8th. The fire burned 10500 acres with no structures destroyed. However, the fire threatened 75 to 100 homes. Mandatory evacuations were ordered for some residents in the path of the fire. Firefighting costs were approximately $2.4M, according to the Carson City District Office, Nevada, Bureau of Land Management.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved northwest to southeast across western Nevada during the evening hours of February 29th into the early morning hours of March 1st. Snow developed behind the front, producing light to moderate amounts of snowfall along the Carson Range and lower valleys of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: For the higher elevations of western Nevada, snowfall around the Carson Range and the Lake Tahoe Basin was generally around 10 to 11 inches above 7000 feet MSL. This snowfall occurred from the evening of 29 February through the morning of 1 March.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved northwest to southeast across western Nevada during the evening hours of February 29th into the early morning hours of March 1st. Snow developed behind the front, producing light to moderate amounts of snowfall along the Carson Range and lower valleys of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: For western Nevada from the evening of 29 February through the morning of 1 March, snowfall in North Reno was generally between 2.5 to 2.7 inches. In Carson City, snowfall of up to 3 inches was reported during the aforementioned time period.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Potent late season winter storm brought periods of heavy snow to the Sierra, with light snow and strong gusty winds to far Western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall anywhere from 0.5 inches to 4 inches around the Reno, Sparks, and Carson City areas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Potent late season winter storm brought periods of heavy snow to the Sierra, with light snow and strong gusty winds to far Western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds along Highway 95 near Walker Lake. Road closed to high profile vehicles.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Potent late season winter storm brought periods of heavy snow to the Sierra, with light snow and strong gusty winds to far Western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall 6-13 inches with highest around Incline Village. No ski area reports due to pandemic shutdowns.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A progressive upper-level trough with an attendant weak atmospheric river brought breezy southwest winds to the lower valleys of western Nevada with light snowfall along the US 395 corridor to more heavy snow along the eastern side of Lake Tahoe. Travel impacts were mainly from the gusty southwest winds 40 to 56 mph, which occurred within the wind-prone areas of US 395 near Stead and Washoe Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Generally, 11 to 14 inches of snow fell above 7000 feet along the east side of the Lake Tahoe basin and Carson Range. Higher liquid water contents were seen at SNOTELs around the area due to higher snow levels and rain at the beginning of the event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A progressive upper-level trough with an attendant weak atmospheric river brought breezy southwest winds to the lower valleys of western Nevada with light snowfall along the US 395 corridor to more heavy snow along the eastern side of Lake Tahoe. Travel impacts were mainly from the gusty southwest winds 40 to 56 mph, which occurred within the wind-prone areas of US 395 near Stead and Washoe Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Light snowfall reports of trace to 1 inch above the valley floor of western Reno and near Stead, with a trace to a half-inch for areas near Virginia City and western Carson City.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of short-wave troughs brought periods of snow to the Carson Range and gusty winds to western Nevada from the 25th through the 26th. Gusty winds and showers began early the 25th ahead of the cold front, followed by post-frontal snowfall the evening of the 25th through the morning of the 26th. Although snowfall amounts were not very heavy, cold temperatures with light accumulations produced difficult driving conditions in the Lake Tahoe Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several areas saw southwest wind gusts as high as 40 to 50 mph. Wind-prone areas along US Highway 395 in the north valleys of Reno saw southwest gusts 50 to 58 mph. For the wind-prone areas along US Highway 395 in Washoe Valley and near Galena, wind gusts as high as 60 to 65 mph were seen.|||A few wind-prone areas within the Sierra Valley and along US Highway 395 saw wind gusts 40 to 46 mph with an outlier gust to 63 mph seen at the Rice Canyon RAWS.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of short-wave troughs brought periods of snow to the Carson Range and gusty winds to western Nevada from the 25th through the 26th. Gusty winds and showers began early the 25th ahead of the cold front, followed by post-frontal snowfall the evening of the 25th through the morning of the 26th. Although snowfall amounts were not very heavy, cold temperatures with light accumulations produced difficult driving conditions in the Lake Tahoe Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: For the higher elevations of western Nevada, snowfall around the Carson Range and the Lake Tahoe Basin was generally around 4 to 10 inches above 7000 feet MSL. Near Incline Village, 2 inches of snow fell.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving storm brought a round of gusty winds and light snow to far western Nevada from the 30th into the 31st. This caused minor travel impacts to these areas during the morning commute on the 31st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very light snowfall up to an inch fell across Reno with a dusting to half an inch falling in the northern portion of Carson Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak cold front brought light snow showers to northern Reno and areas of the northeastern Lake Tahoe Basin. Very minor travel impacts were seen.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Up to an inch of snow fell above 7000 feet in the northern portion of the Carson Range bordering the Lake Tahoe Basin.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak cold front brought light snow showers to northern Reno and areas of the northeastern Lake Tahoe Basin. Very minor travel impacts were seen.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very light snowfall less than an inch fell across portions of north Reno.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving area of low pressure moved into northeast California and the Sierra Nevada producing modest snowfall around the Lake Tahoe Basin and the Carson Range. This brought minor travel impacts to these areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: For the higher elevations of western Nevada, snowfall around the Carson Range and the Lake Tahoe Basin was generally around 4 to 5 inches above 7000 feet MSL.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of short-wave troughs brought periods of snow to the Carson Range and gusty winds to western Nevada from the 25th through the 26th. Gusty winds and showers began early the 25th ahead of the cold front, followed by post-frontal snowfall the evening of the 25th through the morning of the 26th. Although snowfall amounts were not very heavy, cold temperatures with light accumulations produced difficult driving conditions in the Lake Tahoe Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Some strong winds with gusts 40 to 50 mph were seen along the passes of the Carson Range leading into the Lake Tahoe Basin. Along Slide Mountain northeast of Lake Tahoe, gusts as high as 81 mph were seen.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A quick-moving closed upper low brought light snow to areas of far western Nevada from the 27th into the 28th. This lead to slick roads in the Lake Tahoe Basin, western Reno, and western Carson City during the Monday morning commute.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: For the higher elevations of western Nevada, snowfall around the northern portion of the Carson Range and the Lake Tahoe Basin was generally around 2 to 3 inches above 7000 feet MSL.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A quick-moving closed upper low brought light snow to areas of far western Nevada from the 27th into the 28th. This lead to slick roads in the Lake Tahoe Basin, western Reno, and western Carson City during the Monday morning commute.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very light snowfall less than an inch fell across portions of western Reno, western Carson City, and the western portions of the Carson Valley near Gardnerville and US Highway 395.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving storm brought a round of gusty winds and light snow to far western Nevada from the 30th into the 31st. This caused minor travel impacts to these areas during the morning commute on the 31st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: For the higher elevations of western Nevada, snowfall around the northern portion of the Carson Range and the Lake Tahoe Basin was generally around 2 to 3 inches above 6500 feet MSL. Along the south and east shore, up to an inch fell.
